summary: The suite covers all eight TDD cycles and exercises every top-level requirement: INI parsing, comment handling, type coercion (int/float/bool/yes-no), multi-line continuation, schema validation (missing sections, missing keys, wrong types), and JSON/YAML serialization. Organization is clean — Describe/Context/It hierarchy, $script: scoping in BeforeAll, meaningful test names, and inline comments tracing each cycle. Gaps that reduce rigor: (1) Export-ConfigAsJson and Export-ConfigAsYaml have no dedicated unit tests, only integration coverage; (2) no tests for empty/null content passed to the parser; (3) CRLF line endings are handled by the implementation but never explicitly exercised; (4) values containing '=' signs (e.g., connection strings) are untested; (5) duplicate keys within a section have undefined behavior with no test; (6) no AfterAll cleanup of the fixtures directory; (7) the 'float' type in Test-ValueType is never validated directly; (8) error-path behavior for Export functions (bad path, no permissions) is absent. The happy-path and common edge cases are solid, but the missing boundary and error-path tests mean the suite would miss a class of regressions.
